name: Amanaderos de Riodeva Loop
description: Hike the moderate Amanaderos de Riodeva (circular GR 10) trail, a 8.3-mile route featuring stunning waterfalls and diverse terrain.
region_intro: Hiking around Riodeva offers a diverse landscape characterized by forests, water features like waterfalls, and varied terrain. The region includes areas such as the Amanaderos and the unique Forest of Las Blancas. Trails often traverse natural parks and connect to longer-distance routes like the GR 10. This area provides a range of hiking experiences, from moderate forest paths to more challenging ascents. Best hiking trails around Riodeva The most popular hiking route is Amanaderos de Riodeva Loop, a 8.3 miles (13.4 km) trail that takes 4 hours 0 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the Amanaderos area, known for its natural beauty. Another top favourite among local hikers is The Forest of Las Blancas — Circular from Puebla de San Miguel, a moderate 5.2 miles (8.4 km) path. It offers a journey through the unique Forest of Las Blancas. Local hikers also love the Amanaderos Waterfalls and GR 10 Trail from Camarena de la Sierra, a 14.6 miles (23.4 km) trail leading through Amanaderos Waterfalls and sections of the GR 10 Trail, often completed in about 7 hours 28 minutes. Hiking around Riodeva is defined by dense forests, scenic waterfalls, and historic trails. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Riodeva are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.8 stars from over 8 reviews. More than 114 hikers have used komoot to explore Riodeva's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Riodeva?
A: There are over 20 hiking trails around Riodeva, offering a diverse range of experiences from easy walks to more challenging ascents through forests and varied terrain.

Q: Are there hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Riodeva?
A: Yes, Riodeva offers several easy hiking routes perfect for beginners or families. In total, there are 6 easy trails to explore, allowing everyone to enjoy the natural beauty of the region.

Q: Can I find hikes with waterfalls in Riodeva?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its water features, including waterfalls. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974625544/amanaderos-waterfalls-and-gr-10-trail-from-camarena-de-la-sierra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Amanaderos Waterfalls and GR 10 Trail from Camarena de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt;, which leads through the scenic Amanaderos area. The highlight &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4457070" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Amanaderos of Riodeva&lt;/a&gt; is a notable waterfall in the area.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see while hiking in Riodeva?
A: Riodeva is rich in natural beauty. You can discover the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4457129" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Blancas (Juniper Forest)&lt;/a&gt;, explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4457070" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Amanaderos of Riodeva&lt;/a&gt; with its waterfalls, or visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5151605" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Cabrito&lt;/a&gt;. For panoramic views, consider trails leading towards &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3925573" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Javalambre&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2305519" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Javalambre Astrophysical Observatory&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Riodeva?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e822450801/the-forest-of-las-blancas-circular-from-puebla-de-san-miguel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Forest of Las Blancas — Circular from Puebla de San Miguel&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through the distinctive juniper forest. Another great choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10374306" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puebla de San Miguel Village – Las Blancas (Juniper Forest) loop from Puebla de San Miguel&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are the hiking trails around Riodeva dog-friendly?
A: Most hiking trails in natural areas like Riodeva are generally dog-friendly, provided dogs are kept under control, especially around wildlife or livestock. Always carry water for your pet and clean up after them. It's advisable to check local regulations for specific trails or protected areas.

Q: Where can I find parking for the hiking trails in Riodeva?
A: Parking is typically available at or near the trailheads of popular routes. For example, trails starting from Puebla de San Miguel or Camarena de la Sierra usually have designated parking areas. It's recommended to check the starting point of your chosen route for specific parking information.

Q: Is it possible to reach the hiking trails in Riodeva by public transport?
A: Public transport options to reach specific trailheads in Riodeva can be limited, as it is a rural area. While some villages might have bus connections, direct access to all hiking starting points might require a car. It's best to research local bus schedules to nearby towns if you plan to rely on public transport.

Q: What is the best season for hiking in Riodeva?
A: The best seasons for hiking in Riodeva are generally spring and autumn, when temperatures are mild and the landscape is vibrant. Summer can also be pleasant, especially in higher elevations or during early mornings, but it can get warm. Winter offers a different experience, with potential for snow in higher areas, requiring appropriate gear.

Q: Are there challenging hikes for experienced hikers in Riodeva?
A: Yes, Riodeva offers several challenging routes for experienced hikers. There are 5 difficult trails in the area, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974625544/amanaderos-waterfalls-and-gr-10-trail-from-camarena-de-la-sierra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Amanaderos Waterfalls and GR 10 Trail from Camarena de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt;, which covers a significant distance and elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10374309" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puebla de San Miguel Village – Pino la Araña loop from Puebla de San Miguel&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Riodeva?
A: The hiking trails in Riodeva are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 3.8 stars from over 8 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, including dense forests and scenic waterfalls, and the variety of routes catering to different ability levels.

Q: Are there opportunities for wild swimming near Riodeva's hiking trails?
A: While the region features water elements like the Amanaderos waterfalls and various streams, specific designated wild swimming spots are not explicitly highlighted. Always exercise caution and check local regulations regarding swimming in natural bodies of water.
tips: Its name refers to the surge from where the Riodeva is born. Not only is the landscape and environment of the Amanaderos something unique, but so is the path to get here. From the town of Riodeva, follow a path that in sections coincides with the GR 10 and runs along the river ravine between steps, boulevards and footbridges. A route that also runs through several waterfalls depending on the flow of the river depending on the rains.

faqs.question: How difficult is the Amanaderos de Riodeva (circular GR 10) trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is generally considered a moderate hike. While the main GR 10 route has easily accessible paths, some sections require good fitness and sure-footedness due to elevation changes and equipped parts with stairs and handrails. There is also a more challenging "Equipped Amanaderos Trail" variant for those seeking a greater challenge.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The Amanaderos de Riodeva (circular GR 10) typically takes approximately 4 hours and 1 to 25 minutes to complete, covering about 13.4 to 13.7 kilometers.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail is famous for its numerous spectacular waterfalls, including Salto de las Yegüas (22m), Salto de las Ninfas (24m), Salto Polaina (9m), and the impressive Salto de la Virgen Blanca (48m). You'll also encounter diverse landscapes, from dense pine forests to Mediterranean scrub, and pass by an old mill, a pond (balsa), and caves like the "Sebastián Cave." The area is recognized as a "Point of Geological Interest." Don't miss The Amanaderos of Riodeva, the trail's namesake.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Amanaderos de Riodeva (circular GR 10)?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied. You'll hike along a river ravine with geological steps, through dense pine forests, and into Mediterranean scrub. The path consists of natural footpaths, some agricultural roads, and equipped sections with stairs and handrails, especially around viewpoints overlooking the barranco (ravine) and its cascades. You'll also cross a stream multiple times and encounter areas with reddish "rodeno" rock.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to visit is generally during spring or autumn when temperatures are milder and the waterfalls are often at their most impressive due to recent rainfall. Summer can be hot, while winter might bring colder temperatures, though the trail is accessible year-round.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route in the vicinity of Riodeva. Parking is typically available in or near the village. For a smoother experience on this circular route, some hikers recommend organizing with two vehicles, leaving one at the trail's end.

faqs.question: Is the Amanaderos de Riodeva (circular GR 10) trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, given some equipped sections with stairs, handrails, and potentially staples or safety cables, ensure your dog is comfortable with varied terrain and keep them on a leash, especially near steep drops or other hikers.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a permit or have to pay an entrance fee to hike the Amanaderos de Riodeva (circular GR 10). It is an officially cataloged "Sendero Turístico de Aragón," ensuring periodic maintenance and promotion for public access.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the "De Valencia a Lisboa (Etapa Camarena de la Sierra - Riodeva)" and the "Camino de los Huertos" trails. You might also encounter parts of the "Sendero Equipado "Amanaderos"" and "Ruta de las cascadas."

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restaurants near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Riodeva is a small village, and while specific cafes or restaurants directly at the trailhead might be limited, you can usually find options for refreshments and meals within the village itself or nearby towns. It's always a good idea to check local opening hours in advance.

faqs.question: What should I wear and bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and varied terrain, wear comfortable hiking boots with good grip. Bring plenty of water, snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and layers of clothing suitable for changing weather conditions. A first-aid kit is also advisable. If you plan to tackle the more challenging equipped sections, consider gloves for handrails.
